goal: Unify CodeRef semantic systems under one scanner: the main pipeline scan produces canonical ElementData with CodeRef IDs, exports, imports, usedBy, related, and rules; .coderef/index.json remains machine truth; semantic-registry.json and optional source headers become projections.
plan_type_rationale: Rule 4: goal mentions sequential gate keyword ('phase')

phases.name: Phase 1 - Discovery and Compatibility Audit
phases.description: Identify every consumer of ElementData, .coderef/index.json, semantic-registry.json, RAG chunks, and populate semantic behavior before changing output shape.

tasks.description: Audit producers and consumers of ElementData, .coderef/index.json, semantic-registry.json, RAG chunks, graph outputs, and populate --semantic.
Record compatibility constraints and existing output contracts so later phases preserve or explicitly migrate consumers.
Gate Phase 1 on an explicit list of fields, consumers, and compatibility constraints for scanner, populate, RAG, and graph surfaces.

phases.name: Phase 2 - Canonical CodeRef Identity
phases.description: Add canonical CodeRef ID generation to ElementData with configurable line anchoring and consistent relative POSIX path normalization.

tasks.description: Extend ElementData with canonical CodeRef ID fields and an option to include or omit line numbers.
Normalize file paths in .coderef outputs and CodeRef IDs to project-relative POSIX-style paths.
Add tests for CodeRef IDs with and without line numbers and for Windows path normalization.

phases.name: Phase 3 - Canonical Semantic Enrichment
phases.description: Strengthen canonical semantic relationship construction so exports, imports, true reverse usedBy, related, and rules live on enriched ElementData.

tasks.description: Update the pipeline semantic relationship stage so usedBy means files that import or depend on this file, not this file's own imports.
Preserve and initialize exports, imports, related, and rules consistently from canonical ElementData.
Add fixture tests proving reverse usedBy across files and preserving exports, imports, related, and rules.

phases.name: Phase 4 - Semantic Projections
phases.description: Generate semantic-registry.json, source headers, and optional enrichment from enriched ElementData without invoking a separate scanner.

tasks.description: Generate semantic-registry.json from enriched ElementData as a projection of .coderef/index.json.
Refactor optional header generation to consume enriched ElementData and remove misleading used_by behavior based on forward imports.
Refactor LLM enrichment utilities to enrich ElementData projections rather than rescan source files.
Add tests proving semantic-registry.json and optional headers match canonical ElementData.

phases.name: Phase 5 - Populate CLI Update
phases.description: Update populate so production semantic registry generation consumes canonical pipeline data and headers are opt-in projection output.

tasks.description: Replace the separate semantic integration call in populate with canonical projection generation from pipeline data.
Update CLI flags and help text so semantic registry output and optional source-header generation are separate and headers remain off by default.
Add populate fixture tests proving minimal mode writes .coderef/index.json with semantic fields and does not write source headers by default.

phases.name: Phase 6 - Tests and Documentation
phases.description: Complete fixture coverage and documentation for the canonical scanner truth model, semantic projections, and optional headers.

tasks.description: Run focused tests for populate, usedBy, path normalization, CodeRef ID modes, semantic registry projection, optional headers, RAG, and graph consumers.
Update docs to state .coderef/index.json is machine truth, semantic-registry.json is a projection, and headers are optional human projection.
Confirm existing RAG and graph consumers continue to resolve CodeRef IDs and no production path uses the duplicate semantic scanner.

hard_stops.reason: Compatibility audit must be accepted before changing canonical output shape.
CodeRef ID and path normalization contracts must be stable before semantic enrichment changes.
Relationship semantics must be validated before projection utilities consume them.
Projection outputs must be verified before populate behavior changes.
CLI default behavior must be verified before final documentation.
Final tests and docs close the workorder.
